body.title: Pipeline the development process — the post-build checklist becomes dispatchable station-components; builder never grades itself
body.text:
# D-dev-pipeline-stations-2026-07-07
**title:** Pipeline development itself — post-build steps as dispatchable station-components
**date:** 2026-07-07
**status:** open (north-star tier; gated behind current debt-clearing, like D-organizing-question-cascade)

## Core reframing (Akien, 2026-07-07)
Neither CC nor aider 'builds and closes proven' by itself. A sprint is: BUILD (one step) then a POST-BUILD CHECKLIST (sprint/sprint-ticket steps 7-13: cleanup, test, grade, audit, verdict, proof, commit, close, index, savestate). Those steps are the orchestrator DISPATCHING to other things (subagents, grader, audit-* examiners, proof_emitter). So the after-build work is a PIPELINE, and the builder/orchestrator on the front is interchangeable: CC or aider (or any future model) drives the SAME line. Aider self-closing-proven is therefore not a trust flag — it's aider executing the same post-build pipeline CC already executes.

## The invariant that makes it safe: SEPARATION OF POWERS
The builder must NEVER grade its own work — an INDEPENDENT component must. (Supersedes the earlier weaker framing 'the discriminating test must come from upstream/the ticket'.) It doesn't matter that a builder can't self-certify; it shouldn't. Hand the built branch/diff to a TESTER and an EXAMINER that did not write it, and the self-grading-hollow hazard is removed WITHOUT a human in the loop. This is what lets the routine compile away from human supervision.

## The stations (build -> test -> examine -> prove -> merge -> index/savestate)
Each is a rackmount COMPONENT any orchestrator dispatches to (device.py/shim.py; independently deployable):
- BUILD: the model loop (aider/DS). Produces a branch/diff. Does NOT test or grade itself (today aider's runner runs the gate in its clone; that MOVES to the tester).
- TESTER (first to build; T-tester-rackmount): runs the built code's tests in ISOLATION and returns a verdict. OWNS THE TESTING CONTAINER (absorbs D-containers-sandbox-device-2026-07-07): the container is the tester's sandboxed execution environment, not a standalone device. Untrusted model-generated code executes inside the tester's sandbox (CP6), off the host, isolated (imports + FS + net + DB).
- EXAMINER: PARTLY EXISTS -- evaluator/device.py is a 3-voter judge panel (majority verdict); plus the audit-* examiner skills and /critic. Wire these as pipeline stations rather than CC-only skills.
- PROVER: emit_merge_proof (BUILT, T-builder-merge-time-proof) -- emits the HEAD-valid red->green proof at merge so the ticket flips shipped-unproven -> proven.
- MERGER: the MISSING station -- rebase onto HEAD, run the full gate + prover, merge iff green else escalate. (Deliberately scoped out of T-builder-merge-time-proof.)
- INDEX/SAVESTATE/CLEANUP: currently baked into the sprint skill (CC-only); decompose into dispatchable stations.

## Who examines the examiner? (keeping stations honest)
Stations are themselves LLM-based and fallible. What keeps them honest is the SAME thing keeping aider honest: DIVERSITY (independent stations / multi-voter panels -- the judge panel already votes 3-way) + an OUTCOME CORPUS (each station earns trust from its track record against ground truth). Turtles, but CONVERGING -- not turtles all the way down. Per-ticket-class autonomy widens as the corpus proves a class out (gate-removal staircase, project_pipeline_trajectory_gate_removal).

## Relationship to other work
- This is a DIRECT INSTANCE of D-organizing-question-cascade-2026-07-07: the post-build checklist IS a cascade level; each check is an organizing question resolved by a station (extant-pattern -> dispatch the component; novel -> escalate to the expensive resolver).
- Reflexive/self-improving (project_intention_compiler_reflexive, project_self_improving_goal): the dev pipeline is itself a factory whose stations the factory can build and improve -- the factory-of-factories made literal (project_factory_vision).
- ABSORBS D-containers-sandbox-device-2026-07-07: the sandbox container becomes the tester station's isolation substrate, not a co-equal device (avoids torn architecture; one owner).

## Scope / timing
North-star tier; GATED behind current debt-clearing (offload-loop scale + Windows aider + the drain running). First concrete build when the gate opens: the TESTER RACKMOUNT that owns the container. Full per-station decomposition deferred until the vision settles.

## Hypothesis
Decomposing the post-build checklist into independent station-components lets ANY orchestrator (CC or aider) drive the same validation pipeline, and separation-of-powers (builder != grader, enforced by station independence + diversity + outcome corpus) yields objective proven-close for compilable ticket-classes WITHOUT per-ticket human supervision. Falsifiable first slice: the tester rackmount validates an aider-built branch in its container and returns a verdict an independent examiner did not have to be a human to trust; a deliberately-hollow build earns a red verdict from the tester it could not have graded away itself.

## Measurement Signal
A tester rackmount exists, owns its container, and returns test verdicts on aider-built branches; aider's runner no longer runs the gate itself (build/test separated). vs today: testing is ad-hoc (CC subagents / test-fix skill / aider runner shells pytest in-clone on the host) and the builder grades its own work.

## Intention
I intend that development is a pipeline of independent station-components (build, test, examine, prove, merge) that any orchestrator dispatches through, with the builder never grading its own work, so proven-close for routine ticket-classes needs no human in the loop and the genuinely-novel escalates.
